verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is the only IZUMO family member lacking a transmembrane domain; it is a soluble protein. Evidence converges on cytoplasmic/intra-acrosomal localization in testicular cells (spermatocytes), with no TM domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented outer-leaflet surface pool in any cell state. GeneCards reports nuclear localization; Human Protein Atlas shows cytoplasmic staining in seminiferous duct cells. There are no antibody/ADC/CAR-T programs targeting it on the cell surface, and no evidence of cancer-cell ecto-display, cell-death exposure, or wash-resistant surface attachment. No pMHC story exists either. Verdict: cytoplasmic/intracellular soluble protein.
